consistency.notes: All non-null/non-empty ground-truth labels are supported by the page text. Product name, roaster, location, origin, region, process, varieties, producer, altitude, roast, harvest, tasting notes, producer description, price/currency, bag size, package grams, bag count, availability, and booleans are all directly stated or clearly entailed by the page. 'coffee.roaster_country' is supported by 'Leeds, UK' implying United Kingdom.

realism.reasons:
This reads very plausibly as a real roaster wholesale product page. The structure is believable: header/nav, wholesale gating note, product title, tiered pricing, stock/roast schedule, coffee specs, tasting notes, producer/process background, checkout disclaimer, related products, variant block, and footer. The coffee details are coherent and realistic for a Kenyan washed lot: Nyeri, factory/co-op naming, SL-28/SL-34/Ruiru 11, 1,700–1,900 masl, and blackcurrant/grapefruit tasting notes all fit specialty coffee norms. The variant data is also internally consistent: case sizes, total grams, SKUs, and per-bag math all line up.

Minor tells keep it from a 5. 'North Star Coffee Roasters' is a UK roaster, yet prices are shown in USD while the footer says Leeds, UK; not impossible, but a bit odd unless this is a specific export/storefront context. 'AA' is a Kenya screen-size grade and can appear in product names, but some specialty roasters would contextualize it more carefully. The phrase '[default_for_inference]' in the variant data is clearly synthetic/meta rather than something from a real html-to-text rendering. Also, the product copy is slightly too neatly explanatory in places, especially the note clarifying the headline 'from' price, which feels optimized for this prompt. Still, overall it is close to what a real wholesale Shopify-style page could look like.

label.coffee.producer_or_farm: Gichathaini Factory, Gikanda Farmers Cooperative Society
label.coffee.sensory_text:
Tasting notes: Blackcurrant, pink grapefruit, cane sugar

A bright, articulate Kenyan lot for wholesale bars that want a dependable filter offering with plenty of sparkle. In the cup we find blackcurrant cordial, pink grapefruit acidity and a clean cane-sugar finish.
label.coffee.producer_text: Gichathaini Factory is one of the washing stations operated by Gikanda Farmers Cooperative Society in Nyeri. Smallholder members deliver ripe cherry grown on the red volcanic soils around the slopes of Mount Kenya. After pulping, the coffee is fermented, washed in clean water channels and dried slowly on raised beds.
